Ad Text:
Who we are and what we offer:

Interested in shaping skylines across the globe?

Multiplex is a premier construction company with a simple purpose: to construct a better future. As part of our team, you'll have the opportunity to help deliver some of the world's most iconic projects, supported by approachable leadership and an inclusive culture that puts our people first.

You will also have access to a range of holistic benefits including:

Bonus schemes, annual remuneration reviews and salary continuance insurance.
Health and Wellbeing programs, including annual voluntary skin and health checks.
Benefits to support parents and families, including 20 weeks paid parental leave regardless of gender, payment of superannuation on unpaid leave, and transition coaching.
Professional career development opportunities and a wealth of learning offerings through the Multiplex Learning Academy.
An industry-leading approach to flexible work to enable better work-life balance.

Who we're looking for:

Our people are united by a common belief: that we can outperform in everything we do.

We are actively searching for experienced individuals who are looking to further develop their career as building Services Engineers.  This is specifically for our NEXTDC-S4 data centre project, a $4 billion data center in Sydney designed for AI, using 300MW of power & revolutionary liquid cooling.

As a SERVICES ENGINEER OR SENIOR SERVICES ENGINEER within Multiplex, you will assist the Services Manager with the design, testing, installation, completion, and handover of multidiscipline services, ensuring accurate document control and management throughout the process. Additionally, you will manage regular interactions with Suppliers, Consultants, Sub-contractors, Client, regulatory bodies, councils, and industry organisations.

The successful candidate will also hold a tertiary degree in a related technical field, as well as hands-on experience on mid to large scale construction projects. This is a key role on our team, and we are seeking individuals whom are familiar across various disciplines of building services. This includes electrical, mechanical, hydraulics and fire safety.

A note on diversity:

We're committed to building a workplace where people of all genders, cultures, identities, abilities and life experiences feel they belong. We welcome applications from people of all genders, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally diverse communities, LGBTQIA+ communities, people with disability, and veterans. 

You don't need to meet every requirement to be considered so if this role sparks your interest and you want to help build a better future, we'd like to hear from you.  We're committed to a barrier‑free recruitment experience. If you need any adjustments, simply let our Talent Acquisition team know.

#LI-HD1

Are you ready to move beyond standard data center fit-outs and help build the physical backbone of a global AI cloud?

We are representing a world-leading technology firm that is making a multi-billion dollar investment in its global AI capabilities. They are urgently seeking experienced Data Center Designers for a mission-critical infrastructure expansion in Sydney and Melbourne.

This is a rare opportunity to join a high-priority, large-scale project from the ground up and in flight, contributing your expertise to a team that is shaping the future of cloud computing. This is a long-term contract role offering the chance to work on ground breaking projects of immense scale and complexity.

We are hiring for roles from Mid-Career to Lead level across their Sydney and Melbourne offices.

What you’ll be doing:
You will be responsible for creating and managing the design of data center white space, including rack layouts, power distribution, and network infrastructure for a major cloud platform. You will collaborate with internal stakeholders and co location providers to ensure engineering standards are met for this critical global project.

What you'll bring to the team:

Proven professional experience working in critical data center environments.
Experience creating design drawings using AutoCAD/Revit or similar tools.
Strong knowledge of data center electrical and mechanical infrastructure.
PUE experience
Experience MOPs/SOPs/EOPs for carrying out systems operations 
Understanding of BMS/EPMS control systems and data collection/trending.
What else is offered:

A 12-month initial contract with a strong likelihood of extension.
The opportunity to work on a globally significant AI infrastructure project.
Opportunity to get exposure to ground breaking large scale projects.
What's next:
If you're an experienced Data Center Designer looking for a challenging, long-term contract with a world-class team, APPLY NOW!

We can't wait to speak with you.

This role is ideal for someone with strong technical fundamentals who wants to grow into applied ML engineering over time.

What you will be doing?

● Supporting the deployment of data science models into production environments
● Assisting with ML pipelines and model lifecycle processes
● Helping integrate models via APIs so they can be used by other systems
● Learning best practices for ML engineering and model deployment

What we are looking for?

● 0 to 2 years experience or strong graduate project background
● Degree in computer science, software engineering, or similar
● Strong Python skills and working knowledge of SQL
● Interest in ML systems, deployment, and cloud platforms
● Willingness to learn from senior ML and data engineering team members
● Strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset

Experience with GCP, basic ML concepts and backend services and API's will be an advantage

Location: Melbourne

Contract Type: Permanent

MinterEllison is one of Australia’s largest independent law and consulting firms. With a heritage of almost 200 years, we are known for our excellence, and our authentic and enduring relationships with our clients, our people and our communities. Clients seek out MinterEllison to help them solve complex problems every day. We do this by our full-service legal offering and complementary consulting offering.

We offer opportunities to work on industry-leading mandates for top-tier clients, while being part of a high-performing and collaborative team that values excellence, diversity of thought, curiosity, and inclusion. We value our people and empower them to achieve their ambitions - with the support, trust and autonomy to grow their careers in meaningful ways.

At MinterEllison, we're leading the way with AI and other emerging technologies, powering innovation right across our firm. You will have the opportunity to engage with AI tools we've built, and other external AI tools, to enhance efficiencies and excellence internally and with our clients.

We support sustainable ways of working regarding how, when and where you work and offer a wide range of social, financial and health benefits (see https://www.minterellison.com/-/media/Minter-Ellison/Files/Careers/MinterEllison-Employee-Benefits-Dec_2025.pdf)

The Role

The AI & Digital Trainer is a key driver of the firm’s digital transformation, sitting within the Digital group and working closely with project, change and technology teams. This role focuses on accelerating the adoption of AI and digital tools across the firm by translating technology into practical, day‑to‑day value for our people. We are recruiting an AI & Digital Trainer for our Melbourne, Sydney and Brisbane offices with hybrid working arrangements available.

As an AI & Digital Trainer, you will:

Design and deliver high‑quality digital learning solutions aligned to business needs
Coach and support partners, lawyers and business services teams in the effective use of AI and digital technologies
Drive firmwide adoption of AI tools including Legora, Microsoft Copilot, HelpMe (Moveworks), and other emerging technologies
Deliver digital mentoring and coaching programs that support real work scenarios
Build trusted‑advisor relationships with stakeholders across all levels of the firm
Partner with Digital, project and change teams to understand end‑user experiences and remove barriers to adoption
Modernise and enhance existing IT training materials, incorporating innovative and self‑paced learning approaches
Monitor and report on AI and digital adoption metrics, translating insights into actionable recommendations
Contribute to continuous improvement of learning resources and digital capability across the firm
Ensure digital practices align with cyber security policies and technology standards

More About You

You are confident working with senior stakeholders and passionate about helping people adopt new ways of working through technology.

You will bring:

Professional qualifications in training, learning design or a related discipline
5 years + demonstrated experience designing and delivering eLearning and digital learning solutions
Experience coaching professionals in the adoption of AI and digital tools, ideally in a professional services environment
Strong working knowledge of Microsoft 365, AI‑enabled tools and enterprise applications
Understanding of change management principles (e.g. ADKAR or equivalent) and experience managing resistance to change
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with confidence in public speaking
Proven stakeholder engagement and consulting skills, with the ability to extract and translate workflow insights
Strong problem‑solving skills and a customer‑focused mindset
Ability to interpret adoption and usage data and turn insights into practical recommendations
A proactive, self‑starter approach with the ability to manage competing priorities

How to apply

We encourage applications from people of all ages, abilities, cultural backgrounds, genders (including trans or gender diverse), LGBTQ+ people and those with carer responsibilities. We particularly encourage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people to apply.

We prefer to connect with people directly, so please submit your CV by clicking on the 'Apply' button. We encourage all applications, including if you do not meet all the criteria listed for the role. Your application will also enable us to consider you for other opportunities that may be available at MinterEllison.

If you are currently a MinterEllison employee, please apply through the internal careers page.

If you would like further information, require any adjustments throughout the recruitment process or for a confidential discussion, please contact Alexandra.Hussain@minterellison.com.

Senior Integration and Data Engineer, IT
Sydney (Broadway) Campus
2 x Full-time, Continuing opportunities

Friendly and collaborative environment

Rewarding opportunity

$146,765 pa (level 9) + 14.5% superannuation

About the university

The University of Notre Dame Australia is a private Catholic University with over 1,000 permanent staff providing an exceptional educational experience to over 12,000 students across our Fremantle, Broome, and Sydney campuses, as well as clinical schools in Victoria and New South Wales.

About the office

The Information Technology Office is at the forefront of the University’s success, driving cutting-edge technology solutions that empower Learning & Teaching, Research, and Administration across the institution.
 


About the role

As our Senior Integration & Data Engineer, you will be a key contributor to UNDA’s Integration and Data Modernisation initiative. In this high impact role, you will architect, design, deliver, and maintain secure, scalable integration solutions and ETL/ELT data pipelines within our Microsoft Azure environment.

You will act as the solution owner for integration platforms and data pipelines, collaborating closely with IT teams and business stakeholders to shape modern, connected digital experiences. You will also provide technical leadership and oversight to external contractors, ensuring solutions are delivered to a high standard and aligned with our enterprise architecture and security principles.

Your work will be instrumental in integrating complex enterprise systems and business applications across the University-enabling seamless digital experiences, unified master data, and the advanced analytics that power data driven decision-making.

Key Responsibilities

Deliver integration and data solutions aligned with enterprise architecture principles and UNDA’s IT governance model, ensuring scalability, maintainability, and security.

Ensure ETL/ELT processes and data pipelines are reliable, monitored, and meet SLAs while supporting analytics, reporting, and business intelligence needs.

Apply DevSecOps practices to enable secure, automated, and resilient deployment of data and integration services, including infrastructure-as-code.

Support service delivery in line with ITIL practices, focusing on reliability, incident response, change management, and continuous improvement.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams, stakeholders, and external partners, and maintain clear system and user documentation for operational continuity.

Provide mentorship and capability uplift to team members while driving continuous improvement through best practices, modern tooling, and automation.

Qualifications, skills & experience

Postgraduate qualification in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline, and equivalent relevant experience, or an equivalent combination of relevant experience and/or education/training.

Preferred: Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or a closely related field.

Proven experience designing and delivering enterprise-grade, cloud-native data and integration solutions on Microsoft Azure.

Demonstrated experience designing, implementing, and governing integration and data solutions in complex enterprise environments using Microsoft Azure services, with strong capability in ETL/ELT, data warehousing, data lake architectures, SQL/NoSQL, and defining data engineering and modelling requirements for analytics and BI.

Hands‑on expertise with Azure Functions, Logic Apps, ADF, APIM, Azure SQL, Data Lake, Service Bus, Event Grid, Azure DevOps, and Microsoft Fabric.

Proficient in Python and C# and experienced with unit testing, automated testing, CI/CD pipelines, DevSecOps, and writing secure, high‑performance code while applying FinOps principles for cost optimisation.

Proven ability to work across complex organisational structures, build strong cross‑functional relationships, apply data‑driven and risk‑based approaches to prioritise engineering activities, drive continuous improvement and innovation, and communicate technical concepts clearly to stakeholders.

Benefits

Fast paced environment with opportunity for professional growth

Generous training and development opportunities

Friendly and collaborative working environment

How to apply

Please apply online with a resume and cover letter outlining your suitability for the role.

Applications closing date: 18 March 2026

For further information on how to apply, please visit: How to apply | Notre Dame

Applicants are expected to have current and valid work rights in Australia.

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people are encouraged to apply.      

The University of Notre Dame Australia seeks to increase the diversity of our workforce to better meet the different needs of the University and its stakeholders and to improve equal opportunity outcomes for our staff.

Contact us

Please direct your queries regarding this role to Haritha Garlapati, Haritha.Garlapati@nd.edu.au

Unsolicited contact from recruitment agencies will not be entertained.

About Us 
Automic Group brings together modern purpose-built technology with a dynamic and experienced team to tailor solutions for companies and funds to understand, engage and manage their investors. We provide a cloud-native single registry, employee share plan and board management platform, and professional services including company secretarial, ESG, accounting and finance, and investor and media relations. 

Today, over 1,400 ASX listed and unlisted clients trust Automic’s 250 employees to manage their registry, investor and board requirements – and Automic is ranked number one on the ASX by number of companies serviced, with 750 listed clients. In the past two financial years, Automic has won 50%of IPOs and 70% of listed companies who have switched registries. 

Our culture is innately entrepreneurial, comprising people who thrive in an atmosphere where ideas, actions and initiative are encouraged. Our core values, Customer Success, Ambition, Equality and Team are at the foundation of who we are and how we operate.


About the Role

 

This role requires strong software engineering and SQL capabilities, combined with excellent analytical skills and a customer-centric mindset. The engineer will diagnose complex issues, perform safe and auditable data corrections, build tools to streamline support, escalate defects to developers, and contribute to procedures and knowledge base material. Success in this role depends on a solid understanding of software development concepts and strong SQL proficiency.

 

Key Responsibilities include:

 

Platform Support, Troubleshooting & Data Correction

Provide Level 2 support for Automic’s proprietary platform, handling escalations from frontline teams.
Troubleshoot application, database, integration, and configuration issues across multiple components.
Investigate request with SQL queries to identify root causes and required changes.
Perform controlled, auditable data corrections in line with internal governance and change requirements.
Reproduce issues, capture logs, and conduct detailed analysis to isolate defects and behavioural inconsistencies.

Collaboration & Escalation

Work closely with software developers to escalate issues with clear diagnostic evidence.
Collaborate with product, QA, systems, and operations teams during triage and incident management.
Assist with validation and testing of fixes to ensure issues are resolved effectively.

Tooling, Automation & Continuous Improvement

Develop scripts and internal tools to streamline support workflows, reduce manual effort, and improve diagnostic efficiency.
Identify recurring issues and recommend long-term remediation, including improvements to code, monitoring, and processes.
Contribute to platform observability through enhancements in logging and alerting.

Documentation & Knowledge Management

Create and maintain support procedures, troubleshooting guides, and technical runbooks.
Build and expand the internal knowledge base to enable faster and more accurate resolutions.
Ensure all documentation remains accurate, version-controlled, and aligned with governance and ISMS requirements.

Customer Engagement

Provide customer-focused support with clear, timely, and professional communication.
Explain technical findings in a business-friendly way to customers and internal stakeholders.
Participate in customer calls when required to clarify issues, impact, and resolution paths

 

 


About you!

 

To be successful in this role, we are looking for someone with:
Strong background in software engineering or application/platform support.
Solid SQL skills, including writing, debugging, and optimising queries.
Practical understanding of APIs, REST services, logs, and application-level debugging.
Ability to read and interpret code (Java, C#, Typescript, JavaScript, Python or similar).
Experience supporting complex, custom-built systems.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a methodical approach.
Excellent communication skills and a customer-centric attitude.
Proven experience writing high-quality technical documentation.
Experience in financial services, fintech, or other regulated environments.
Exposure to AWS, Docker, microservices, or modern CI/CD pipelines.
Familiarity with monitoring tools such as CloudWatch, Sumo Logic, Datadog, or Prometheus.
Understanding of ITIL or similar operational frameworks.
Why join Automic:

You’ll be part of a team where operational excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement are genuinely valued. This is an opportunity to deepen your expertise, contribute to meaningful outcomes, and grow your career within a high-performing, purpose-led fintech environment.

Key Benefits include:

Hybrid working
Professional career growth & development
Supportive & fast paced environment
Opportunity to grow with the business

If the above sounds like you, please apply for this exciting opportunity and be part of the growth journey with Automic!

About Us – Making Healthcare Friendlier

HeartBug is the friendliest heart monitoring service in Australia! We are growing fast and constantly expanding, so there are many opportunities for the future.

We really care about providing the very best service to our customers – clinics, hospitals, doctors, cardiologists and patients. We are enthusiastic and highly motivated. We have a friendly, easy-going and relaxed office, where we don't take ourselves too seriously and enjoy ourselves while delivering excellent service both internally and externally.

Our Headquarters are in Sydney's Eastern Suburbs, Hillsdale.




About the Position

We are opening two positions for skilled AI Engineers with a strong foundation in signal processing, data science, and machine learning to join our healthcare technology team.

In this role, you will contribute to our mission of delivering world-class cardiac monitoring through advanced AI-driven solutions by designing, building, and deploying machine learning enabled models that improve arrhythmia diagnostic efficiency and support technicians and Electrophysiologists in preparing monitoring reports.

Working alongside healthcare and technology experts, you will bring scalable AI models into production, ensuring their reliability and performance in real-world applications.

These positions offer the flexibility of remote work for candidates who are onshore in Australia with valid Australian working rights, and we are pleased to offer full-time, part-time, or casual arrangements.




How to Apply – Impress with your letter

Yes, send your resume BUT more importantly, write a one-page cover letter with as many reasons you can think of detailing why we should hire you and why you are the perfect addition to our dream team!

There’s never been a more exciting time to shape the energy transition. As a Digital Innovation Engineer, with a strong software and analytics stack, you’ll play a pivotal role in transforming how an electricity distribution business operates by designing, building and delivering scalable, data-driven solutions that improve safety, performance and customer outcomes.

This role will require you to work closely with different teams across Endeavour Energy as we work together to deliver safe, reliable and affordable power to our customers.

About the Role

This is a highly technical, hands-on role for an engineer who thrives on solving complex operational and business challenges but also enjoys working closely with business stakeholders. You’ll identify inefficiencies, uncover data gaps and build end-to-end digital solutions that automate processes, enhance decision-making and unlock measurable value across the organisation.

From advanced analytics, python scripting, AI/ML to digital twins and automation, you’ll apply modern technologies to real-world engineering problems and see your solutions in action.

What You’ll Do

Analyse operational, engineering and business processes to identify improvement opportunities and define clear technical problem statements
Design and deliver end-to-end digital solutions from data ingestion and transformation through to analytics, automation and visualisation
Build dashboards, analytics tools and decision-support systems that drive operational performance, safety and productivity
Develop automation solutions to eliminate manual processes and improve data quality and workflow reliability
Apply emerging technologies (Digital Twins, AI/ML, advanced analytics, robotics) to practical engineering challenges
Apply advanced programming and Python skills to analyse LiDAR and imagery datasets, delivering high-quality spatial insights to support engineering outcomes
Think outside the box with innovative and creative solutions as well as strong problem solving skills
Partner with stakeholders to understand requirements, validate assumptions and iteratively deliver business value
Continuously improve digital products based on performance insights and user feedback
Establish reusable tools, standards and patterns to uplift digital capability across the organisation
Stay at the forefront of emerging technologies and industry innovation

About You

You’re analytical, commercially aware and naturally curious. You enjoy breaking down complex problems, working across disciplines and influencing others to embrace smarter ways of working.

You bring:

3–10 years’ experience in a similar professional or technical role
A Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Data Science or a related discipline

Technical capability in:

SQL, Python, Azure Analytics, Databricks
Power BI, Knime, FME or similar analytics platforms
Data modelling, automation and workflow design

You’ll also demonstrate:

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, applying innovative solutions
The ability to manage multiple concurrent projects
Clear and persuasive communication skills to handle stakeholder management
Commercial awareness and understanding of enterprise impacts
A solid understanding of electricity/energy utility operations

Why Join Us?

Work on meaningful projects that power communities
Deliver innovative solutions with real operational impact
Collaborate with engineers, analysts and business leaders
Help shape the digital future of energy

If you’re passionate about combining engineering, data and innovation to solve real-world problems — we’d love to hear from you.

For further information, please contact recruitment@endeavourenergy.com.au

Applications close – 25 March 2026. 

When we power together, we can achieve so much more.

At Endeavour Energy, having a diverse and inclusive workplace makes us better, safer, and stronger. We strive to create an environment where everyone feels safe, motivated, valued, included and respected.

Endeavour Energy is a critical infrastructure organisation and performs various background checks to evaluate the suitability of an applicant before making an employment offer to ensure the security and protection of its assets.

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

The team

You will join the Artificial Intelligence Platform Services (AIPS) & Analytics team, comprising over 250 members across the Asia-Pacific region. This team is structured into cross-disciplinary squads, bringing together professionals from Data Science, Product, Engineering, and Data Analysis to collaboratively research, design, build, and support end-to-end AI-based services that deliver direct value to hirers and job seekers. The safe and responsible use of data and AI is a cornerstone of the team's mission, driving efforts to make employment markets fairer, more accessible, transparent, and efficient for everyone.  

To learn more about SEEK’s approach to Responsible AI, please visit SEEK’s Responsible AI page: https://www.seek.com.au/about/responsible-ai

About the role 

This Senior Data Scientist role sits within the Artificial Intelligence Platform Services (AIPS) team and reports to the Head of Responsible AI. You will be a mixture of a Data Scientist, a Machine Learning Engineer, and a Researcher.  Successful performance in this role will be demonstrated by developing insightful research and analyses on AI-based products and proposing and implementing changes to deliver positive social impact. Furthermore, the successful candidate will be expected to stay up to date with emerging techniques and methodologies in Responsible AI, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Machine Learning, and be deeply involved in a culture of continual improvement and collaboration.  Someone passionate about solving practical and impactful challenges in the intersection areas of a multi-disciplinary field to build long-term sustainable, Responsible AI businesses. You will have the opportunity to shape the future of Responsible AI at SEEK by leveraging our governance processes, developing and distributing best practices, and driving innovation. 

Responsibilities 

Partner with squads to embed Responsible AI into AI/ML and analytics product design and delivery.
Design, test, and continuously improve Responsible AI assessment methods, tools, and metrics collaboratively.
Run robust experiments on AI-based services and iterate data products with Strategy, Product, Data Science, and Engineering.
Develop and consolidate Responsible AI guidelines, playbooks, training, and supporting materials.
Support squads in integrating Responsible AI processes into the AI development lifecycle.
Advocate for Responsible AI/ML best practices, clearly communicating benefits and limitations to stakeholders.
Gather, model, and validate relevant data; contribute to research planning, experimental design, and problem-solving across APAC teams.
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

3+ years’ experience building and improving data-informed, internet-scale products.
Postgraduate qualification in a quantitative or behavioural science discipline  (e.g. Physics, Mathematics, Bioinformatics, Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics)
Strong knowledge of Responsible AI/AI Ethics frameworks and AI risk concepts.
Solid foundations in algorithms and data structures, plus ML, IR, recommender systems, LLMs, NLP, or HCI.
Expertise in data analysis, statistics, and experimentation (online and offline).
Proficiency in Python and data visualisation/EDA tools (e.g., Databricks, Tableau).

Job Description


About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

What you’ll do

Lead and grow a high-performing team of Machine Learning Engineers focused on automating content review and moderation across Canva’s Content Library.

Set and execute the technical vision and strategy for ML-driven safety, quality, and policy enforcement across text, image, video, audio and template content.

Design and scale robust ML systems — including experimentation frameworks, evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and feedback loops to continuously improve model performance and close the gap between manual review and automation.

Partner cross-functionally with Content, Product, Review Ops, and Review Engineering to deliver impactful, user-facing solutions and scalable human-in-the-loop systems.

Drive large, end-to-end initiatives from problem framing and roadmap definition through to delivery, experimentation, rollout, and impact measurement.

Uplift engineering excellence through strong technical direction, pragmatic architecture decisions, and a healthy code review and knowledge-sharing culture.

Coach and mentor engineers to navigate complex technical challenges and do the best work of their lives.

Champion operational excellence, responsible AI, and safety-by-design, ensuring our systems serve all users, including students and educators, with quality, integrity, and trust.

You're probably a match if

You collaborate cross-functionally and thrive in partnerships with product, ML, and content stakeholders.

You think strategically and deliver pragmatically, balancing long-term vision with immediate needs.

You value diversity, empathy, and inclusion, and lead in a way that fosters belonging and trust.

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Overview

With more than 45,000 employees and partners worldwide, the Customer Experience and Success (CE&S) organization is on a mission to empower customers to accelerate business value through differentiated customer experiences that leverage Microsoft’s products and services, ignited by our people and culture. We drive cross-company alignment and execution, ensuring that we consistently exceed customers’ expectations in every interaction, whether in-product, digital, or human-centered. CE&S is responsible for all up services across the company, including consulting, customer success, and support across Microsoft’s portfolio of solutions and products. Join CE&S and help us accelerate AI transformation for our customers and the world.

The Global Customer Success (GCS) organization, an organization within CE&S, is leading the effort to enable customer success on the Microsoft Cloud by harnessing leading, AI-powered capabilities and human expertise to deliver innovation solutions that accelerate business value, drive operational excellence and nurture long term loyalty.

We are looking for a Global Solution Architect (CSA), specializing in Copilot, who is passionate about driving our customers’ AI transformation on the Microsoft platform.  This role will be part of the Customer Success AI Business Solutions team and is designed to strategically engage in the execution of consumption within key accounts. This role will coach and collaborate with field roles and partners, executing programmatic actions that drive the consumption and AI transformation. This role will document and share best practices, ensure progress towards value realization goals, provide feedback to the Product Group, and facilitate knowledge transfer to field Customer Success Unit (CSU) teams. Additionally, the role involves partnering with field teams to identify key barriers to scaled success and driving resolution with engineering, product marketing, and sales teams. 

Step into the future of business transformation with our M365 Copilot and Agents incubation team! Here, you’ll be at the forefront of innovation, working as a Global Solution Architect to shape and validate the next generation of AI-powered business solutions. You’ll collaborate with engineering and business leaders to design, test, and refine cutting-edge use cases, leveraging real-world customer scenarios to drive product-market fit and create tangible learnings for the entire organization. Your ideas will directly influence the evolution of Microsoft 365 Copilot and Agents. 

What sets this role apart is its focus on incubation—where experimentation, rapid learning, and bold thinking are not just encouraged, but essential. You’ll join a select team tasked with validating early-stage solutions, capturing breakthrough use cases, and building the ROI methodology that will define the future of our platform. You’ll operate at the intersection of engineering and business, aligning with a global list of “frontier” customers and codifying best practices that will scale worldwide. If you thrive in ambiguity, love to create clarity from complexity, and want to be part of a team that’s setting the standard for how AI transforms work, we want you to help lead this journey! 

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Galvanize technical and sales experts around additional identified opportunities to develop customer specific roadmaps that drive further growth & business value realization.

Lead business & technical conversations with customers to drive value from their MS investments. Deliver all work according to MS best practices & policies and using repeatable IP.

Job Title: Senior Data Scientist - ML and AI

Your new team - The Faculty of Engineering and Information Technology
The Faculty of Engineering and Information Technology (FEIT) has been the leading Australian provider of engineering and IT education and research for over 150 years. We are a multidisciplinary Faculty organised into three key areas; Computing and Information Systems (CIS), Chemical and Biomedical Engineering (CBE) and Electrical, Mechanical and Infrastructure Engineering (EMI). The Opportunity

You will join a high-performing Network Technology team responsible for designing, automating and supporting large-scale, mission-critical network environments across cloud, data centre and enterprise domains.
This role will suit a hands-on engineer who enjoys building automation frameworks, improving operational efficiency and working across multi-vendor environments.

Key Responsibilities
Develop and maintain network automation and orchestration solutions, including proof-of-concept initiatives
Design and implement automation workflows across multi-vendor environments
Collaborate with network architects, SMEs and stakeholders to deliver automation-driven improvements
Identify and implement appropriate orchestration frameworks, APIs and infrastructure components
Integrate automation with existing cloud and on-prem environments
Support lifecycle management across network platforms
Contribute to documentation, knowledge sharing and continuous improvement initiatives
Ensure automation reliability, performance and service delivery outcomes
About You
You will bring strong hands-on experience in network automation and DevOps practices within complex environments.

Required Experience
4+ years in Network Automation or Network DevOps roles
Experience across at least two of the following:

AWS networking
Enterprise or Data Centre networks
ISP/Carrier environments
Security appliances (firewalls, load balancers, etc.)
Strong Python skills for automation, data parsing and API integration
Experience building or maintaining automation frameworks at scale
Deep understanding of network protocols and configurations (e.g. BGP, VXLAN, ACLs, F5 VIPs, SSL, PKI)
Experience with AWS networking (Transit Gateway, VPC, Direct Connect, Route 53, ALB/NLB, etc.)
Orchestration tooling such as Ansible, SaltStack, Cisco NSO or NetBox
Infrastructure-as-Code tools such as Terraform
CI/CD integration (GitLab, GitHub Actions, Jenkins)
REST APIs, JSON/YAML
Version control using Git
Multi-vendor exposure (Cisco, Arista, Palo Alto, etc.)
Please contact Matthew Nicholas at Lanson Partners for more information. matthew@lansonpartners.com.

About Us

Founded in 2013, Payroll Metrics delivers cloud-based payroll and workforce management software. Our platform integrates seamlessly with HR, finance, LMS, banking, and gateway systems to streamline payroll for Australia and New Zealand businesses. We're ISO 27001, ISO 27701 and ISO 9001 certified, demonstrating our ongoing commitment to data security and service excellence. Our workplace culture values respect, collaboration and continuous improvement. We believe in a solutions-focused, no-blame approach that fosters innovation and accountability.

About the Role

Payroll Metrics is seeking a versatile Infrastructure & AI Engineer to join our technology team. This remote role bridges the gap between modern cloud infrastructure and cutting-edge artificial intelligence, focusing on the scalable, secure deployment of AI-driven payroll solutions. Reporting directly to the Infrastructure & AI Operations Manager, you will be instrumental in the platform's transition to a high-performance Azure architecture.

This position is designed for an individual passionate about building the "data factory" that powers our AI strategy. You will collaborate closely with the Manager to ensure a seamless knowledge transfer of our AI-driven architecture, reducing key-person risk while driving initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:

Design and implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and agentic workflows using Azure AI Foundry to power internal and client-facing AI tools.

Architect and optimise specialised Vector DB storage to enable high-speed semantic search for AI initiatives.

Configure and manage Azure Private Link and private endpoints.

Perform domain-specific fine-tuning and optimisation of LLMs to accurately interpret complex AU/NZ payroll legislation.

Build backend logic and AI integrations using C#, .NET 8 and Python to enhance the core Payroll Metrics platform.

Manage the deployment and scaling of AI models and supporting services using Containers for consistent cross-environment performance.

Support the management of Azure SQL Elastic Pools, ensuring multi-tenant data isolation and high performance.

Implement "Privacy by Design" controls to maintain our ISO 27001/27701 certifications and ensure all AI processes are audit-ready and PII-secure.

Collaborate with the Infrastructure & AI Operations Manager to document architectures and internalise system logic, serving as a critical backup for platform operations.

Candidate Profile

This is a mid-level role designed for a technically proficient engineer who has mastered the fundamentals of cloud infrastructure and is now looking to specialise in the operationalisation of AI. You should have a proven track record of managing production environments and a strong desire to transition into an "AI-first" engineering mindset.

3–5 years of experience in a DevOps, Systems Engineering, or Backend Development role, with at least 1–2 years specifically focused on Azure cloud services.

Strong proficiency in Python for automation and data handling, alongside a working knowledge of C# and the .NET framework to support our platform migration.

Demonstrable experience managing Azure PaaS (App Services, SQL Elastic Pools) and deploying applications via Containers (Docker/AKS).

A solid understanding of RAG architectures and experience working with LLM APIs. You should be familiar with the concept of Vector Databases and how to manage data retrieval for AI context.

Advanced SQL skills with the ability to manage complex queries.

Experience with Git and Azure DevOps pipelines, with a focus on building automated workflows.

Experience with Agent frameworks and Model Context Protocol (MCP) is preferred and considered a valuable asset.

Key Attributes

Architectural Thinker: Balances immediate needs with long-term scalability and security.

Collaborative Communicator: Capable of transferring technical knowledge and bridging silos between IT and development.

Security-Minded: Prioritises data residency (Australia) and "Privacy by Design" in every implementation.

Why Join Payroll Metrics?

At Payroll Metrics, employees are part of a purpose-driven SaaS organisation delivering essential payroll and workforce solutions across Australia and New Zealand. As the company continues to scale, it is investing in secure, innovative systems and this role offers a unique opportunity to help lead that transformation.

The successful candidate will work within a skilled and collaborative team in a position that bridges cutting-edge AI, cloud infrastructure, and tangible impact across the technology stack. Payroll Metrics values initiative and lifelong learning and offers strong support for professional growth and development, including direct mentorship from senior leadership to master the next generation of sovereign AI technology.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:



Agentic Data Engineer | Audit & Assurance Data & Digital | Sydney

Work in a start-up like environment - opportunity to develop new features
Flexible work arrangements - work in a way that suits you best
Holistic well-being program - flu vaccinations and health & wellbeing focused work

We are seeking a talented engineer to join our Audit & Assurance Data & Digital team in Sydney. This is a permanent opportunity to help design and scale next-generation AI-enabled audit solutions across Deloitte Asia Pacific.

Sitting at the intersection of audit, data engineering and AI system design, this role will allow you to shape production-grade agentic systems that operate at scale across thousands of clients. This is not experimental AI - this is engineering for defensibility, auditability and enterprise performance.

What will your typical day look like?
You'll work at the intersection of audit, data engineering and AI architecture, translating complex audit workflows into scalable, automated Python and SQL pipelines. One day you might be modelling messy multi-client datasets; the next, designing how LLM reasoning integrates with deterministic, auditable rule engines.
You'll spend your time hardening and evolving production systems - strengthening validation, refining orchestration logic, improving performance and packaging reusable AI "skills" that can be deployed consistently across engagements. You'll collaborate closely with audit, asset and engineering teams to ensure our AI-enabled workflows are scalable, transparent and robust.

About the team
The Audit & Assurance (A&A) Data & Digital team is at the heart of Deloitte's Asset Transformation Strategy across Asia Pacific.
We are a multidisciplinary group of data technologists, designers, developers and management consultants building proprietary big data platforms that support thousands of clients and engagements. Our platform processes billions of records across hundreds of solutions throughout Australia and Asia Pacific, and we are continuously evolving it to deliver AI-enabled, enterprise-grade capabilities at scale.

Enough about us, let's talk about you.

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

2-5 years of experience building production-grade workflow, data or AI automation systems in multi-client environments

Strong Python software engineering skills (modular design, testing, packaging, async workflows)

Experience designing scalable data pipelines using Python (Pandas or Polars) and SQL

Experience working with messy, high-variance datasets

Experience implementing automated data quality checks, reconciliation logic and validation frameworks

Understanding of structured outputs, JSON schema enforcement and Pydantic validation

Experience integrating LLM-based systems (RAG, tool calling, structured output pipelines)

Experience handling unstructured document data (PDF parsing, OCR, embeddings, classification)

Exposure to CI/CD tooling such as Azure DevOps, GitHub or Jenkins

A self-starter mindset, comfortable working autonomously in a fast-paced and dynamic environment

Exposure to accounting or regulatory data structures, AI-assisted development tools, Tableau/PowerBI or Databricks is advantageous but not essential.

Why Deloitte?
At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.
We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.
We support flexibility and choice. We encourage you to find the right balance between connecting in person with your clients and teams and meeting your own personal needs.
We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.

Next Steps

Sound like the sort of role for you? Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

#LI-Hybrid

By applying for this job, you'll be assessed against the Deloitte Talent Standards. We've designed these standards so that you can grow in your career, and we can provide our clients with a consistent and exceptional Deloitte employee experience globally. The preferred candidate will be subject to background screening by Deloitte or by their external third-party provider.

About the Role

We’re building a small, agile digital hub to simplify processes, connect systems, and apply AI where it makes sense. To do that, we’re looking for a hands-on Automation and AI Solutions Specialist who loves turning ideas into practical solutions to deliver real impact.

This role is about making things smarter and faster: automating repetitive tasks, connecting systems, and embedding AI where it delivers real business value. You’ll be prototyping, experimenting, and delivering solutions that help staff and customers in weeks, not months.

What you’ll be doing

Designing and implementing automation across operational, marketing, and creative systems.

Building and managing integrations between Microsoft 365, Dynamics 365, SharePoint, Dataverse, and external SaaS platforms.

Creating solutions in Power Apps to improve internal and customer-facing workflows.

Using APIs (REST, Microsoft Graph, GraphQL, SOAP, webhooks) to enable connectivity.

Embedding AI capabilities using Azure OpenAI, Microsoft Copilot, and other AI services to enhance processes and insights.

Rapidly prototyping solutions using AI-assisted development tools (e.g., Cursor, Claude).

Analyse processes and identify where automation or AI can deliver measurable value.

Testing, documenting, and refining solutions to ensure they are reliable and maintainable.

Exploring emerging technologies to continuously improve operations.

What we’re looking for

Strong skills in Power Automate, Power Apps, and Power Platform integration.

Strong scripting/programming skills (Python, JavaScript or TypeScript).

Hands-on experience with automation and integration across Microsoft 365 and Azure.

Knowledge of APIs (REST, GraphQL, SOAP), data flows data integration (MS other services).

Web development skills (React, Node.js, HTML/CSS, Tailwind).

Experience with SQL/NoSQL databases.

3+ years’ experience in automation, integration or development roles.

Bonus points for

Experience with Azure services (Azure OpenAI, Azure ML, Azure DevOps).

Exposure to AI/ML APIs (OpenAI, Google AI, Hugging Face).

Cloud platform experience (Azure preferred, AWS or GCP also valued).

Knowledge of containerisation or DevOps practices (Docker, Kubernetes).

Certifications in automation, cloud, or integration technologies.

Interest in LLMs, process mining, or Microsoft Fabric analytics capabilities.

Why join us?

You’ll be part of a small, innovative team where your work has real impact.

If you’re curious, pragmatic, and excited to explore AI in real-world ways, we’d love to hear from you.

About The Borg Group

The Borg Group is a leading privately-owned Australian enterprise with a strong presence in manufacturing, recycling, and property development. Founded in 1989 by brothers John and Michael Borg, the company is headquartered in Somersby, New South Wales, and employs nearly 3,000 people across Australia and New Zealand.

Borg is committed to environmental responsibility, actively integrating sustainable practices into its operations. Initiatives such as reDirect Recycling and Direct Pallets play a key role in reclaiming materials like used formwork, trusses, plywood, particleboard, and pallets. These efforts not only reduce landfill waste but also contribute to the production of recycled-content particleboard.

Innovation is a cornerstone of Borg’s success. The company continually invests in automation and advanced technologies, driving new product development and maintaining its position at the cutting edge of the industry. Its world-class manufacturing facilities are a testament to this forward-thinking approach.




Borg is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Ad Text:
AI and Digital Enablement Lead
Location: Sydney
Job Type: Full-time
Salary: upto130,000 Package
Start Date: As soon as possible

Position Summary

This is a national AI/ Digital enablement role responsible for driving the adoption, capability uplift and use of AI and digital technologies to shape how 2,000 people work in the AI era. This is a national role responsible for turning AI strategy into real behaviour change across seven service lines. You will build the networks, programs and capability frameworks that make AI adoption stick at scale.

What you will own

Enterprise AI adoption at scale, leading the national rollout of M365 Copilot, Power Platform and AI tools through a high impact Champions Network and Communities of Practice.
Digital capability uplift, designing structured learning pathways, bootcamps and workshops that build genuine digital fluency across the firm.
Program leadership, translating strategic direction into clear enablement plans, measurable adoption outcomes and consistent national delivery.

What you will lead

A Technology Adoption Specialist, providing coaching, direction and quality oversight.
A national Champions Network embedded across divisions, reducing reliance on central IT and accelerating peer-led adoption.
Cross divisional engagement with senior leaders, shaping tailored adoption plans aligned to client delivery.

What you bring

3+ years in technology adoption, change, digital enablement or learning in a corporate or professional services environment.
Previous experience managing Microsoft 365, Copilot and Power Platform.
Prior consulting background
Experience building communities of practice or champion networks at scale.
Confidence facilitating workshops with senior stakeholders and translating strategy into structured programs that deliver measurable results.
Strong commercial judgement, clear writing, and the ability to operate independently in a national role.
Experience working across multiple business units or divisions with competing priorities.
Ability to work independently and translate strategic direction into structured programs without close supervision.

You will not set AI strategy or governance. You will make it work in practice.

If you are energised by scaling behaviour change, building digital capability and leading from the front in AI adoption, this is your opportunity to define how a national firm works in the next decade.

Only applicants with full Australian work rights and previous experience with generative AI experience will be contacted.
